ogTitle: Lockhart River vs Lockyer: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lockhart River and Lockyer.
heading: Lockhart River vs Lockyer — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lockhart River, Queensland and Lockyer, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lockhart River has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lockyer (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lockhart River or Lockyer safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lockhart River has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Lockyer scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lockhart River scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Lockyer scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lockhart River or Lockyer?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lockhart River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
